title: lasthenia ferrisiae
intro: Lasthenia ferrisiae, also known as Ferris's goldfields or Ferris' goldfields, is a rare annual plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the sunflower family and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Lasthenia ferrisiae can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers are used in traditional herbal remedies.
